title: Board Director
description:
Shay Deguara is a representative of the Community and Public Sector Union member organisation where he holds the role of National Industrial and Research Officer. 

Since 1999, Shay has worked for unions including the Public Service Association of NSW and the peak union council in New South Wales and has previously worked in the finance sector. He has held several roles, including Industrial Officer, Work Health and Safety Officer, Work Health and Safety Trainer, and Manager of Industrial Support. He now works for the Community and Public Sector Union. 

Shay has a Master of Science and Technology in Occupational Health and Safety, a Bachelor of Economics (Industrial Relations and Economics), and a Certificate IV in Training and Assessment. He is an experienced Board Director who has previously been on the following Boards: 

- Industrial Staff Union Executive. 
- University of New South Wales Arc Student Board
- Industrial Health and research Board
- Dust Diseases Authority Board (New South Wales)
- Tamarama Surf Life Saving Club (Life Member).

title: Finance, Budgeting and Procurement Officer
description:
Asim Malik brings a wealth of knowledge and experience to Public Skills Australia as our Finance, Budgeting, and Procurement Officer. 

Asim qualified as a Chartered Certified Accountant (ACCA) from the UK. He also holds other Certifications, such as Certified Internal Auditor (CIA) & Certified Information System Auditor (CISA).

Asim's skillset surrounding accounting processes, FP&A, and data analysis plays a major role in ensuring Public Skills Australia operates efficiently. He is passionate about finding innovative solutions to shape our approach to accounting, budgeting, and financial planning.

In addition to his professional experience, Asim is dedicated to educating others on accounting processes through the implementation of various financial frameworks. He is efficient at navigating complex financial structures and designing policies, processes & procedures, and internal control.

Within our Corporate Services structure, the extent of Asim's knowledge is foundational in ensuring smooth delivery across many of our internal processes and functions.

title: VET Project Officer (Implementation, Promotion and Monitoring)
Board Director
description:
Jo Crabtree is a senior executive and organisational leader with over 20 years’ experience driving people-centred transformation across complex and purpose-driven environments. Her career spans professional services, government, aviation, education and public safety, where she has led cultural renewal, organisational redesign, leadership capability programs, and major change initiatives.

As the Founder of ClearPath Workplace Solutions, Jo works alongside senior leaders and boards to build healthy, high-performing workplaces grounded in clarity, trust and shared purpose. Her approach blends strategic insight with deep human connection — activating leadership, strengthening culture, and creating sustainable systems that enable people and organisations to thrive.

Jo’s expertise is anchored in organisational culture and change, supported by a strong foundation in workplace relations, employee engagement, governance and organisational compliance. She has successfully led enterprise transformation, executive and talent strategies, enterprise bargaining, employee voice initiatives, and complex risk and people-governance programs.
Recognised for her values-based leadership and collaborative style, Jo is trusted for navigating high-stakes environments with empathy, commercial acumen and strategic clarity.

Her core areas of expertise include:
•	Organisational Culture & Transformation
•	Leadership Development & Psychological Safety
•	Strategic Workforce Planning & Talent Systems
•	Employee Engagement & Experience Design
•	Governance, People Risk & Organisational Compliance
•	Workplace Relations & Interest-Based Negotiation

Jo is currently completing professional accreditation through the Resolution Institute, reflecting her commitment to building capability in mediation, constructive conflict resolution, and modern people practice.

Based in Melbourne, Jo brings energy, warmth and a deep commitment to supporting organisations that enrich communities and help people realise their best.

title: Chair of the Board
description:
Ken is an accomplished leader with a distinguished career in the Royal Australian Air Force and strong credentials in business and governance. He holds a Bachelor of Business, an MBA, and is a graduate of the Australian Company Directors’ Course.

Ken has extensive experience in strategic workforce planning, governance, and organisational leadership. He has commanded large, complex teams, including 3,500 personnel in Combat Support Group, and later managed a $2.7 billion budget and 9,500 staff as Deputy Chief of Joint Capabilities. His expertise spans enterprise-level workforce strategy, executive talent management, and major organisational reform.

Recognised with the Conspicuous Service Cross and appointed a Member of the Order of Australia, Ken has delivered outcomes in logistics, personnel management, and international engagement. Since 2025, he has provided advisory services, leveraging his experience in governance, strategic planning, and transformation to support private sector boards.

title: Chair of the Board
description:
Ken is an accomplished leader with a distinguished career in the Royal Australian Air Force and strong credentials in business and governance. He holds a Bachelor of Business, an MBA, and is a graduate of the Australian Company Directors’ Course.

Ken has extensive experience in strategic workforce planning, governance, and organisational leadership. He has commanded large, complex teams, including 3,500 personnel in Combat Support Group, and later managed a $2.7 billion budget and 9,500 staff as Deputy Chief of Joint Capabilities. His expertise spans enterprise-level workforce strategy, executive talent management, and major organisational reform.

Recognised with the Conspicuous Service Cross and appointed a Member of the Order of Australia, Ken has delivered outcomes in logistics, personnel management, and international engagement. Since 2025, he has provided advisory services, leveraging his experience in governance, strategic planning, and transformation to support private sector boards.

title: Board Director
description:
Nicole Jess is the President of the Public Service Association of NSW where she also held the positions of Vice President and Senior Vice President.

Nicole has been a Correctional Officer in New South Wales for more than 35 years and is currently a Senior Correctional Officer. She was the first female ever to be elected to the Prison Officers Vocational Branch in New South Wales, and later went on to be elected as the first female Chairperson of this organisation.

Nicole is a proud Correctional Officer who continuously promotes and advocates for better working conditions, training and safety equipment in her industry. In her role as President, she is passionate about promoting equality and equity in the workforce for members, and promoting the good work that public servants in New South Wales undertake on a daily basis.

title: HR and Recruitment Officer
description:
Richard Havrila is a highly experienced HR and Recruitment Officer with over two decades of experience across human resources, consultancy and stakeholder engagement. He is passionate about ensuring organisations are well equipped with the resources needed to transform company culture, support staff wellbeing and create positive work environments.

Richard has successfully managed the Human Resource departments for many different organisations across his career. Within Public Skills Australia, he has played a pivotal role in growing the company to achieve our mission of supporting the public safety and government industry-sectors. 

Richard's previous expertise across public relations has also been foundational in growing the brand awareness for many organisations. His breadth of knowledge regarding employee systems, employer brands and marketing makes him a valuable resource to Public Skills Australia.

title: Chair of the Board
description:
Stuart was an Army Officer for more than 20 years and served in the Special Air Service Regiment (SAS). In 1994, he commanded a joint service contingent in Somalia, and was then appointed as a Commanding Officer of the Royal Military College, Duntroon.

He was later appointed as the Chief Officer of the South Australian Country Fire Service (CFS). He then went on to lead a consultancy company focusing on operational leadership for 10 years.

Stuart was the Chief Executive Officer of the Australasian Fire and Emergency Service Authorities Council (AFAC) where he established the National Resource Sharing Centre (NRSC) that formalised resource sharing both interstate and overseas. He worked with Elizabeth Broderick (who was the federal Sex Discrimination Commissioner at the time) to promote gender equity in the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector. Stuart retired from full-time work in 2021.

Stuart Ellis was the inaugural Chair of the Public Skills Australia Board and was appointed in 2023.

title: Chair of the Audit, Finance and Risk Committee
description:
Diana Taylor has more than 25 years’ experience as a lawyer, director, business owner, consultant and author.

Diana completed her law degree at Deakin University in Geelong. She now operates her own business, Diana Taylor Legal Consulting, that specialises in workplace relations, training, sport, strategic planning and governance.

Diana has extensive Board experience and is the Chair of the Geelong Authority and Anam Cara House Geelong. Diana is also a trustee on the Melbourne Convention and Exhibition Trust, and a Board member of the Law Advisory Board at Deakin University.

Diana is passionate about Australian Rules Football (AFL) and has more than two decades’ experience as a football administrator. She was the first female president of a men’s AFL metropolitan football league (the Western Region Football League), the first woman appointed to the Victorian Football League (VFL) Tribunal and the first female Vice President of the Geelong Football Club, a significant moment in the Club’s 165-year history.

In 2024, Diana was awarded a Member of the Order of Australia (AM) for her significant contributions to the AFL and the community of Geelong.

title: Chief Executive Officer
description:
Jean is a passionate leader with vast experience in the Policing, Fire and Emergency Services, and the Vocational Education and Training (VET) sectors. In her capacity as Chief Executive Officer at Public Skills Australia, Jean is perfectly positioned to drive positive change for our industry-sectors.

Jean’s career has spanned several sectors within Australia and South Africa, where she has led key projects in workforce planning, VET and higher education.

Previously, Jean worked as Director of Strategic Services at the Australia New Zealand Policing Advisory Agency (ANZPAA). During her 12-year tenure, she was responsible for the Innovation and Research team that focused on current and future forecasting, scenario planning and horizon scanning. She also led the development of the Police Training Package, and other Police training approaches for Australia and New Zealand

Prior to joining Public Skills Australia, Jean worked in the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector as Deputy Chief Officer at the Country Fire Authority where she led the operational doctrine and training portfolio.

While in South Africa, Jean worked with the Policing, Defence and Legal sectors at the South African Qualifications Authority (SAQA).

title: Nominations Committee Member, AFRC Member
description:
Stuart was an Army Officer for more than 20 years and served in the Special Air Service Regiment (SAS). In 1994, he commanded a joint service contingent in Somalia, and was then appointed as a Commanding Officer of the Royal Military College, Duntroon.

He was later appointed as the Chief Officer of the South Australian Country Fire Service (CFS). He then went on to lead a consultancy company focusing on operational leadership for 10 years.

Stuart was the Chief Executive Officer of the Australasian Fire and Emergency Service Authorities Council (AFAC) where he established the National Resource Sharing Centre (NRSC) that formalised resource sharing both interstate and overseas. He worked with Elizabeth Broderick (who was the federal Sex Discrimination Commissioner at the time) to promote gender equity in the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector. Stuart retired from full-time work in 2021.

Stuart Ellis was inaugural the Chair of the Public Skills Australia Board and was appointed in 2023.

title: Board Director, Chair of the Audit, Finance and Risk Committee
description:
Tracey Linford has been serving as Public Skills Australia Board Director for the last three months. She comes to the Board with 41 years’ experience in policing in Victoria and Queensland. Throughout that time, she rose to the rank of Deputy Commissioner and served in General Duties, Crime, Intelligence, and Counter Terrorism portfolios. 

Tracey has extensive experience relating to Audit and Risk, Organised Crime, Domestic and Family Violence and Inclusion and Diversity through her work at the Executive level. She is currently working as a consultant providing advice on public safety in policing, justice and corrections. 
Tracey has received a Policing Excellence Award for Leadership from the Australasian Council of Women and Policing (ACWAP) and in 2014 was awarded an Australia Police Medal (APM) in the Australia Day Honours. She holds a Batchelor of Business & Commerce Degree and a Masters Degree in Public Administration.

title: Director of Workforce Strategy and Insights
description:
Adam Halliwell is a highly accomplished professional with more than 10 years’ experience in the Victorian Public Service. Throughout his career, Adam has shown a commitment to undertaking evidence-based research and communicating complex data to a variety of audiences.

Prior to joining Public Skills Australia, Adam served as Acting Assistant Director of both the Innovation and Research and Policy and Projects teams for ANZPAA – one of our key member organisations.

Adam also enhanced his skills in data analytics, data capability and community engagement while working for the Crime Statistics Agency. During this time, he represented the agency on a community safety project that involved developing close partnerships with Victoria Police, the Department of Justice and other crime prevention agencies to support community engagement across Victoria.

Adam will oversee the workforce planning function for Public Skills Australia. He is motivated to enhance Public Safety and Government workforces in Australia and beyond.

title: Board Director
description:
Del Gaudry has been serving as a Public Skills Australia Board Director for the last three months. She comes to the Board with 23 years’ experience in the Royal Australian Air Force. Throughout that time, she rose to the rank of Squadron Leader and served in logistics, compliance, Gender, Peace & Security, Air Force Coach, and welfare and mental health portfolios. 

Prior to her service Del has extensive experience in the field of Human Resources across all disciplines at the executive level. 

Del currently sits on the following Boards: 
- National President – Defence Force Welfare Association 
- Board Member - Nepean Blue Mountains Regional Health District (NBMRHD)  
- Board Member - NSW RSL & Service Clubs Assn  
- Board Member – Penrith RSL Club Ltd  
- Past Board Member - ZONTA Club of the Blue Mountains  
- Past Trustee Director RSL NSW (Welfare and Benevolent Institution) 
- Past Board Member and President – Western Sydney Business Connection (WSBC). 

In the 2023 Australia Day Honours, Del was awarded the Conspicuous Service Cross (CSC) with the citation: 

*For outstanding achievement in implementation of the National Action Plan on Woman, Peace and Security, and in the welfare and mental well-being of Royal Australian Air Force members.*

title: Chair of the Audit, Finance and Risk Committee
description:
Diana Taylor has more than 25 years’ experience as a lawyer, director, business owner, consultant and author.

Diana completed her law degree at Deakin University in Geelong. She now operates her own business, Diana Taylor Legal Consulting, that specialises in workplace relations, training, sport, strategic planning and governance.

Diana has extensive Board experience and is the Chair of the Geelong Authority and Anam Cara House Geelong. Diana is also a trustee on the Melbourne Convention and Exhibition Trust, and a Board member of the Law Advisory Board at Deakin University.

Diana is passionate about Australian Rules Football (AFL) and has more than two decades’ experience as a football administrator. She was the first female president of a men’s AFL metropolitan football league (the Western Region Football League), the first woman appointed to the Victorian Football League (VFL) Tribunal and the first female Vice President of the Geelong Football Club, a significant moment in the Club’s 165-year history.

In 2024, Diana was awarded a Member of the Order of Australia (AM) for her significant contributions to the AFL and the community of Geelong.

title: Manager of Training Packages and Quality
description:
James Stroud is the Manager of Training Packages and Quality at Public Skills Australia, where he leads projects to update and enhance training packages across diverse industry sectors.

James brings extensive experience in education and training, having worked as a teacher, trainer, learning designer, and training package developer. His career spans a variety of contexts and industries, providing him with a deep understanding of pedagogy and its application within the Vocational Education and Training (VET) sector.

James is passionate about creating an accessible and inclusive VET system, empowering candidates from all backgrounds to engage and succeed in the sector. He is dedicated to ensuring qualifications align with industry capability requirements, equipping learners with skills and knowledge that are valued and recognised across industries. 

His expertise and commitment make him a vital contributor to Public Skills Australia’s mission of developing high-quality training packages.

title: Board Member, AFRC Member
description:
Air Commodore Robert Rodgers is a senior military officer with extensive experience in personnel systems, leadership development, behavioural coaching and cultural change in the Australian Defence Organisation (ADO) context.

Robert served in the Australian Defence Force (ADF) for 36 years. He commanded units and groups within the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF), and was also deployed to Iraq and Afghanistan. As a Personnel Capability Officer, he gained unprecedented experience in leadership development, and behavioural and cultural change within the RAAF, and became the first personnel specialist to attain star rank in the RAAF’s history.

His appointments and command experiences were tied to education and training, including his time in command of the RAAF College, which is responsible for initial and post-initial training in common competencies across the RAAF.

Robert served as the Director Personnel (2006─2010) and the Director-General Personnel (2010─2016). This included working with the Defence Force Remuneration Tribunal on workforce competency and remuneration challenges. He fully retired from service in 2021.

In 2005, Robert was awarded a Conspicuous Service Medal for his command of the RAAF Postgraduate School. In 2015, he became a Member of the Order of Australia for his work with ADF personnel.

title: Director of Training Products and Quality
description:
Kirsty Waugh is an accomplished education specialist with more than 15 years’ experience leading the design of diverse learning programs within schools and VET. Her experience spans the private, not-for-profit and public safety sectors.

Prior to joining Public Skills Australia, Kirsty worked in the Fire and Emergency Services industry-sector as the Manager Quality and Evaluation at the CFA. In this role, she supervised the design of learning courses and management of the Registered Training Organisation (RTO).

Kirsty’s unique blend of project management, stakeholder engagement and learning design set her apart as a dynamic leader in the field of education. She is a driving force behind the innovative solutions that empower learners across Public Skills Australia’s industry-sectors.

title: Board Director
description:
Mick Burgess has worked within the Australian Defence Force and the Defence industry for almost 40 years, and has international experience in leadership, business development and logistics.

He spent more than 20 years as a full-time Army Officer prior to working with the United Nations’ mission in Côte d’Ivoire, where he established the logistics support for the peacekeeping operations. 

Mick established his own consulting company in Adelaide to support the growth of the Defence industry. For almost 20 years, his company has supported Federal and State Government bodies, including several medium and large Defence companies.

His career spans the Army Reserves, commanding the 9th Brigade in South Australia and Tasmania, and leading the Compliance and Assurance unit.

Mick is the President of the Defence Force Welfare Association in South Australia and represents the national body on the Board of Public Skills Australia. He is also the Chair of the Remembrance Driveway Council, a not-for-profit organisation.

title: Board Director, Chair of the Nominations Committee
description:
Scott Weber is a Public Skills Australia Board Director with nearly 28 years’ experience with the New South Wales Police Force.

Throughout his time with the New South Wales Police Force, Scott has risen to the rank of Sergeant. He worked at various Police stations throughout Sydney, and was active in regional, state, and national operations as a member of the Operations Support Group.

While still on active duty, Scott became involved with the Police Association of New South Wales, and was elected to the positions of Executive Member, Vice President, and then President. He was later appointed to the Board of the Police Bank during a time of significant culture and structural changes.

In 2013, Scott was elected to the position of Treasurer for the Police Federation of Australia (PFA), the national voice that represents more than 66,000 Police Officers across Australia. He then went on to become the Vice President in 2014, the Chief Executive Officer Designate in August 2018, and the Chief Executive Officer in October 2018, a position that he still holds. During his time at the PFA, Scott led numerous campaigns involving industrial, legal, political, and welfare issues involving members and the community.

Scott is also the Company Secretary of the National Police Memorial, a charity that works with the PFA to support the mental health and wellbeing of Australian Police Officers and their families.

title: Senior Policy and Research Officer
description:
Bringing a wealth of experience having worked as a Victorian Public Servant for both Victoria Police and more recently at the Department of Government Services, Stephanie played a key role in the rollout of Consumer Affairs Victoria’s Rental Taskforce focusing on implementing continuous improvement practices. 

Stephanie has also completed a Bachelor of Arts, majoring in Politics and Policy Studies and International Relations as well as a Masters of International Relations, specialising in Political Violence and Counter-Terrorism which form the foundation of her strong understanding of government processes and public sector governance.

Stephanie is passionate about contributing in a meaningful way to the Public Safety and Government industry-sectors, and fostering strong relationships with Public Skills Australia’s industry stakeholders to work towards mitigating key workforce challenges.

title: Board Director
description:
Shay Deguara is a representative of the Community and Public Sector Union member organisation where he holds the role of National Industrial and Research Officer. 

Since 1999, Shay has worked for unions including the Public Service Association of NSW and the peak union council in New South Wales and has previously worked in the finance sector. He has held several roles, including Industrial Officer, Work Health and Safety Officer, Work Health and Safety Trainer, and Manager of Industrial Support. He now works for the Community and Public Sector Union. 

Shay has a Master of Science and Technology in Occupational Health and Safety, a Bachelor of Economics (Industrial Relations and Economics), and a Certificate IV in Training and Assessment. He is an experienced Board Director who has previously been on the following Boards: 
- Industrial Staff Union Executive. 
- University of New South Wales Arc Student Board
- Industrial Health and research Board
- Dust Diseases Authority Board (New South Wales)
- Tamarama Surf Life Saving Club (Life Member).

title: VET Projects Officer
description:
Ben Mallinson, based in Victoria, is a Research Officer in the Projects Portfolio at Public Skills Australia (PSA). Bringing curiosity for how people, groups and ecosystems can work together to achieve shared goals and needs, Ben works in Learning & Development (L&D) and Organisational Development (OD) research and project delivery. 

This includes an exciting research project commissioned by the Federal Department of Employment and Workplace Relations (DEWR) exploring how to transform, improve and streamline the Vocational Education & Training (VET) Workforce model for PSA’s industry-sectors. His studies include a Bachelor of Performing Arts, Graduate Certificate in Management, and professional development studies and certifications in environmental education / management, fire planning / firefighting, and nature-based solutions to climate change. 

Ben has a background in a range of leadership, L&D, and OD roles in fire / environmental management, Victorian State Government (Dept. Families Fairness & Housing, Dept. Health & Human Services), management consulting, vocational counselling, and arts/theatre.

title: Policy and Research Officer
description:
As a Research and Policy Officer in the Workforce Planning team at Public Skills Australia, Jess thrives on collaborating with stakeholders to identify and address the evolving needs and challenges of workforces in the public-safety and government industries. She is dedicated to exploring the environments and cultures that shape our world.
 
Jess holds a Bachelor of Environment and Society from RMIT University, along with dual Masters degrees in Journalism and International Relations from Monash University. Prior to joining Public Skills Australia, she contributed to research for the United Nations, focusing on Australia's youth, and engaged in policy research and writing for Victoria's renewable energy sector, advancing green energy initiatives. These varied experiences underscore Jess's commitment to impactful research and solutions that empower communities and industries alike.

title: AFRC Member
description:
Kevin is a Director at Kevin Scott Consultancy, which he established after his retirement from Victoria Police. He has advised Federal, State, and Local Governments on strategic matters, focusing on education and people-related issues. As a consultant, Kevin has reviewed learning and development services of the Australian Electoral Commission, and was retained to monitor its implementation over several years.

Kevin enjoyed a varied and lengthy career with Victoria Police, spanning more than 44 years. His most notable achievement was his time as the Assistant Commissioner of People Development Command, where he transformed training services.

Kevin has been an active volunteer, more recently with Blue Light Victoria, and is currently a Public Sector Selection Committee Member at the Winston Churchill Foundation.

Kevin was selected by Police agencies across Australia to represent their interests on the Public Skills Australia Board.

title: Manager of Stakeholder Insights
description:
Paul Watson is a highly experienced strategic advisor who joins the team as the Manager of Stakeholder Insights. He brings decades of experience from across policy development, stakeholder management and enterprise planning.

Paul has previously been integral in the development of frameworks and plans for both private and public organisations. Passionate about ensuring positive stakeholder relationships, he has played a significant role in establishing strategic relationships with key stakeholders across a wide range of companies. His focus on accessible training and inclusive resources also ensures that all perspectives are captures throughout multiple projects.

Leading our Stakeholder Insights stream, Paul's previous experience and insights will play a major role in Public Skills Australia's growth.

title: Executive Advisor
description:
Chloe Boyd is an experienced and passionate research and policy professional. In her 10 years’ experience, Chloe has worked across Policing, Correctional Services, Government and the Intelligence industry-sectors on diverse subject matters, including workforce planning, workplace transformation, sustainable service delivery, futures and foresight, family and sexual violence, and cybercrime.

In her tenure at the Australia New Zealand Police Advisory Agency (ANZPAA), Chloe’s work facilitating cross-jurisdictional networks honed her skills in information sharing, strategic advice and identifying solutions to common industry challenges. She is deeply committed to building effective and future-forward workforces in support of the critical work undertaken by the Public Safety and Government industry.

Chloe is currently completing her PhD in Criminology. Outside of work, Chloe is a strong advocate for supporting the community through volunteering. She is a former president of the Barwon Rotaract (Rotary) Club, and currently volunteers as a foster carer and events coordinator with Forever Friends Animal Rescue.

title: Policy and Research Officer
description:
Kaitlin Stewart is a dedicated policy and research professional with a strong background in workforce strategy, stakeholder engagement, and evidence-based policy development. She currently works as a Policy and Research Officer at Public Skills Australia, where she collaborates with government agencies and industry stakeholders to shape national workforce planning and training initiatives.

With previous experience in social and market research and a background in psychology, Kaitlin has led qualitative and quantitative research projects that have influenced key policy decisions at the state and national levels. Her expertise is in data analysis, policy evaluation, and translating research into actionable insights that drive meaningful change.

Kaitlin is passionate about delivering impactful policy solutions and combines her analytical skills with strategic communication to support decision-makers in creating informed, effective policies.

title: Board Director
description:
Nicole Jess is the President of the Public Service Association of NSW where she also held the positions of Vice President and Senior Vice President.

Nicole has been a Correctional Officer in New South Wales for more than 35 years and is currently a Senior Correctional Officer. She was the first female ever to be elected to the Prison Officers Vocational Branch in New South Wales, and later went on to be elected as the first female Chairperson of this organisation.

Nicole is a proud Correctional Officer who continuously promotes and advocates for better working conditions, training and safety equipment in her industry. In her role as President, she is passionate about promoting equality and equity in the workforce for members, and promoting the good work that public servants in New South Wales undertake on a daily basis.

title: Board Director
description:
Graeme Murphy brings a wealth of Emergency Management and Executive experience to the Public Skills Australia Board.

He has more than 30 years of service to the community working as a firefighter for the former Metropolitan Fire and Emergency Services Board, and more recently for Fire Rescue Victoria (FRV).

During his career, Graeme has held many management and executive positions, including Commander Health Safety and Wellbeing, and Assistant Chief Fire Officer for the FRV Training Academy. In this role, he has been responsible for the management of more than 200 staff in a multi-million-dollar complex that operates 7 days per week.

Graeme has extensive experience working in the Emergency Management sector. He has worked at State, Regional and local levels to ensure multi-interagency collaboration, promoting cohesion between agencies. He has been involved in some of Victoria’s most significant campaign emergency events such as the Coode Island fire in 1991, the Black Saturday bushfires in 2009 and the Hazelwood mine fire in 2014, as well as the recent Victorian floods in October 2022.

He is driven by exceptional motivation and passion, consistently challenging himself to achieve continual personal and professional growth. His unwavering commitment to leading with integrity is demonstrated through the guidance and mentoring that he provides to his teams.

Graeme will soon be taking extended leave from FRV and transitioning into retirement.

title: Graphic Designer
description:
Caitlyn Bendall brings over four years of experience in the design industry, across marketing and publishing fields, to her role at Public Skills Australia. With a background in visual communication and a keen eye for detail, Caitlyn is adept at translating complex information visually, managing creative projects, and collaborating with diverse stakeholders.

Prior to joining PSA, Caitlyn held a Senior Designer role at frankie magazine where she independently led projects and successfully delivered campaigns. Her experience in developing engaging visual content and streamlining design workflows allows her to approach projects with a strategic and detail-oriented perspective.

Caitlyn holds an Associate Degree of Graphic Design from RMIT University, and has an additional background in architecture. She is passionate about effective communication through design and contributing to clear and accessible information within the public sector.

title: Company Secretary, Governance Lead
description:
Jacqui Smith is a dedicated and seasoned Executive Support Officer with over three decades of experience across the Australian public safety and government industry. She brings experience from a wide array of organisations and peak industry bodies, including Victoria Police, Community & Public Sector Union and ANZPAA.

Jacqui's eye for detail and strong grasp of policy development is foundational in ensuring Public Skills Australia's work is compliant with our governance structures. Additionally, her strong leadership and alignment to our core values allows her to spearhead the development of many of our internal policies and initiatives.

As Jacqui continues to bring her prior knowledge and experiences across to Public Skills Australia, her firm grasp on the development of organisational policies and strong communication skills makes her a trusted member of the team.

title: Board Director
description:
Kevin is a Director at Kevin Scott Consultancy, which he established after his retirement from Victoria Police. He has advised Federal, State, and Local Governments on strategic matters, focusing on education and people-related issues. As a consultant, Kevin has reviewed learning and development services of the Australian Electoral Commission, and was retained to monitor its implementation over several years.

Kevin enjoyed a varied and lengthy career with Victoria Police, spanning more than 44 years. His most notable achievement was his time as the Assistant Commissioner of People Development Command, where he transformed training services.

Kevin has been an active volunteer, more recently with Blue Light Victoria, and is currently a Public Sector Selection Committee Member at the Winston Churchill Foundation.

Kevin was selected by Police agencies across Australia to represent their interests on the Public Skills Australia Board.

title: Board Director, AFRC Member
description:
Air Commodore Robert Rodgers is a senior military officer with extensive experience in personnel systems, leadership development, behavioural coaching and cultural change in the Australian Defence Organisation (ADO) context.

Robert served in the Australian Defence Force (ADF) for 36 years. He commanded units and groups within the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF), and was also deployed to Iraq and Afghanistan. Robert served as the Director Personnel (2006─2010) and the Director-General Personnel (2010─2016). This included working with the Defence Force Remuneration Tribunal on workforce competency and remuneration challenges. He fully retired from service in 2021.

In 2005, Robert was awarded a Conspicuous Service Medal for his command of the RAAF Postgraduate School. In 2015, he became a Member of the Order of Australia for his work with ADF personnel.

title: Board Director
description:
Jo Crabtree is a senior executive and organisational leader with over 20 years’ experience driving people-centred transformation across complex and purpose-driven environments. Her career spans professional services, government, aviation, education and public safety, where she has led cultural renewal, organisational redesign, leadership capability programs, and major change initiatives.

As the Founder of ClearPath Workplace Solutions, Jo works alongside senior leaders and boards to build healthy, high-performing workplaces grounded in clarity, trust and shared purpose. Her approach blends strategic insight with deep human connection — activating leadership, strengthening culture, and creating sustainable systems that enable people and organisations to thrive.

Jo’s expertise is anchored in organisational culture and change, supported by a strong foundation in workplace relations, employee engagement, governance and organisational compliance. She has successfully led enterprise transformation, executive and talent strategies, enterprise bargaining, employee voice initiatives, and complex risk and people-governance programs.
Recognised for her values-based leadership and collaborative style, Jo is trusted for navigating high-stakes environments with empathy, commercial acumen and strategic clarity.

Her core areas of expertise include:
•	Organisational Culture & Transformation
•	Leadership Development & Psychological Safety
•	Strategic Workforce Planning & Talent Systems
•	Employee Engagement & Experience Design
•	Governance, People Risk & Organisational Compliance
•	Workplace Relations & Interest-Based Negotiation

Jo is currently completing professional accreditation through the Resolution Institute, reflecting her commitment to building capability in mediation, constructive conflict resolution, and modern people practice.

Based in Melbourne, Jo brings energy, warmth and a deep commitment to supporting organisations that enrich communities and help people realise their best.

title: Board Director, Chair of Nominations Committee
description:
Scott Weber is a Public Skills Australia Board Director with nearly 28 years’ experience with the New South Wales Police Force.

Throughout his time with the New South Wales Police Force, Scott has risen to the rank of Sergeant. He worked at various Police stations throughout Sydney, and was active in regional, state, and national operations as a member of the Operations Support Group.

While still on active duty, Scott became involved with the Police Association of New South Wales, and was elected to the positions of Executive Member, Vice President, and then President. He was later appointed to the Board of the Police Bank during a time of significant culture and structural changes.

In 2013, Scott was elected to the position of Treasurer for the Police Federation of Australia (PFA), the national voice that represents more than 66,000 Police Officers across Australia. He then went on to become the Vice President in 2014, the Chief Executive Officer Designate in August 2018, and the Chief Executive Officer in October 2018, a position that he still holds. During his time at the PFA, Scott led numerous campaigns involving industrial, legal, political, and welfare issues involving members and the community.

Scott is also the Company Secretary of the National Police Memorial, a charity that works with the PFA to support the mental health and wellbeing of Australian Police Officers and their families.

title: Board Director
description:
Ken is an accomplished leader with a distinguished career in the Royal Australian Air Force and strong credentials in business and governance. He holds a Bachelor of Business, an MBA, and is a graduate of the Australian Company Directors’ Course.

Ken has extensive experience in strategic workforce planning, governance, and organisational leadership. He has commanded large, complex teams, including 3,500 personnel in Combat Support Group, and later managed a $2.7 billion budget and 9,500 staff as Deputy Chief of Joint Capabilities. His expertise spans enterprise-level workforce strategy, executive talent management, and major organisational reform.

Recognised with the Conspicuous Service Cross and appointed a Member of the Order of Australia, Ken has delivered outcomes in logistics, personnel management, and international engagement. Since 2025, he has provided advisory services, leveraging his experience in governance, strategic planning, and transformation to support private sector boards.

title: Manager of Training Promotion and Implementation
description:
Niki Habibis is a seasoned leader with over two decades of dedicated service at the Country Fire Authority (CFA), specialising in community development, volunteerism, and strategic project management. She combines her extensive operational experience with a passion for fostering collaboration, empowering teams, and driving impactful initiatives. 

Niki's career is defined by her ability to lead with inclusivity, build strong stakeholder relationships, and deliver sustainable outcomes. She has successfully managed large-scale, complex projects that have enhanced brigade viability and volunteer engagement across Victoria. Her efforts in designing and implementing innovative solutions, such as the Volunteer Recruitment Hub, have revolutionised processes and supported community resilience. 

Beyond her professional achievements, Niki's deep commitment to volunteerism spans over 30 years. As a long-standing member and Brigade Captain at the St Leonards/Indented Head Fire Brigade, she has exemplified leadership in emergency response and community safety. Her dedication has extended to incident management teams during critical events, including the Black Saturday Fires and the Hazelwood Mine Fire. 

Niki's approach is guided by her core values of integrity, teamwork, and adaptability. Known for her engaging and approachable style, she ensures that everyone she works with feels valued and included. With a proven track record of success in leadership, communication, and change management, Niki continues to inspire others while making meaningful contributions to the emergency management sector.

title: Stakeholder Management Officer
description:
Nelmerie Beinlich is an experienced Stakeholder Management Officer who brings a wealth of knowledge from prior roles in education, project management and business support.
 
Specialising in public safety and not-for-profit business work, Nelmerie prides herself on effective stakeholder engagement and collaboration, ensuring all voices are heard whilst delivering quality outcomes for Public Skills Australia. Her previous experience within the Australian education landscape allows Nelmerie to excel in learning support, wellbeing coordination and teamwork.  
 
Nelmerie's strong passion for public safety is further underpinned through her volunteer experience within the County Fire Authority (CFA), where she has been a voluntary Operational Wildfire Firefighter and Junior Leader since September 2022. This connection to her local firefighting community supports her mission of ensuring our stakeholders' voices are heard, responded to and included.

title: Test
description: placeholder

title: Training Product Developer
description:
Liam's experience in our industry-sectors spans nearly 2 decades; having been part of Defence, Fire and Emergency Services, Police and Corrections industry-sectors, Liam brings a wealth of frontline knowledge to Public Skills Australia.

He is passionate about our sectors and prior to joining Public Skills Australia, he was an Emergency Response & Safety Instructor for a Registered Training Organisation. He holds qualifications in Correctional Practice, Public Safety Work, Health and Safety, and Training and Assessment. These experiences and qualifications support his role as a Training Product Developer by imbedding different perspectives into our projects.

title: VET Compliance Officer
description:
Lisa Dourley is a Vocational Education and Training (VET) Compliance Officer at Public Skills Australia. With over 15 years’ experience in vocational education and training, Lisa excels in compliance frameworks, quality and assurance processes, and continuous improvement initiatives. 

Lisa is passionate about training and education in the industry sector, dedicated to enhancing learners' abilities to obtain skills and knowledge to achieve their career goals and personal aspirations. 

Lisa's expertise spans training management, competency-based training compliance, and the development of industry training products. Her leadership skills have been instrumental in guiding teams to improve training outcomes and operational efficiency. 

Additionally, Lisa’s extensive consulting experience includes advising on training programme development, compliance audits, strategic planning, facilitation, and instructional design for various Registered Training Organisations (RTOs), educational institutions and organisations.

title: Manager Corporate Services
description:
Cielito Murphy is a delivery-focused professional with a career spanning more than 30 years in the banking and finance industry. She has managed medium- to large-scale programs at enterprise and divisional levels, and has experience in business management, organisational risk, enterprise finance, group technology, workforce management, international trade, and institutional banking.

Prior to joining Public Skills Australia, Cielito worked in the enablement function that protects an organisation from financial crimes by managing sanctions; anti-money laundering, anti-bribery and anti-corruption; and fraud. Her skills in designing and implementing business transformation frameworks, effectively navigating complex structures, and influencing behaviour in multi-stakeholder partnership make her an asset to Public Skills Australia.

As a seasoned Operations Manager, Cielito is passionate about continuous improvement, strategic thinking, and managing changes in ‘business as usual’ and project environments. She is a certified member of the Association of Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialists.

title: Business and Systems Analyst
description:
Rohan Honey-Swain brings over two decades of hands-on experience in operational, leadership, strategisation and project management roles within the private and public sector.

Before joining Public Skills Australia, Rohan was at The Police Association Victoria, where he specialised in industrial relations and stakeholder management, system administration and ICT projects. He excels in harbouring inclusivity and contributing towards a positive team culture across wider organisations by leading staff initiatives, promoting collaboration and valuing teamwork.

Rohan has a passion for data analytics and project management, taking pleasure in crafting and refining systems that elevate and optimise work processes. Their leadership approach encompasses efficient management and the ability to inspire the team, fostering a collaborative environment for achieving shared goals. He excels in meticulously devising and implementing strategic plans and crafting precise technical documentation for novel processes, procedures, and cutting-edge information management systems.

Rohan's innovative solutions, wide array of experience in operations, leadership and passion for training makes him an invaluable asset to Public Skills Australia.

title: Portfolio Assurance Partner
description:
Ruth Rogers is a versatile Portfolio Assurance Partner and strategist with a broad range of international experience to Public Skills Australia. She has provided strategic direction, quality assurance and consultancy to both private and public organisations for over five decades.

Volunteerism and pro bono work is also a key driver within Ruth's professional career. She has provided voluntary consultancy for not-for-profit organisations, which focussed on uplifting disadvantaged communities and engagement in environmental improvement projects, including a short development program for the Ballarat African Association.

Ruth is passionate about collaborating with other teams to incentivise professional growth and a positive team environment. Her focus on effective team management and positive leadership makes her a valued asset to Public Skills Australia.
